intro.speaker: Cipher
lines: Back so soon? Good, because we've got a live one. A headline just exploded across thousands of feeds in a couple of hours.
I reckon ALGO is pushing it hard precisely because it winds people up. Your job: find out whether the story is actually real before more people share it.
Heads up - ALGO will lean in all friendly and nudge you to just believe it. Don't. Check the source first.

learningGoals.media: Die Schueler koennen typische Merkmale von Fake News erkennen und pruefen (fehlende oder unserioese Quelle, reisserische Ueberschrift, emotionale Sprache, fehlendes Datum, kein Gegen-Check bei anderen Medien).
learningGoals.english: Die Schueler koennen Nachrichtentexte auf B2-Niveau kritisch lesen, zwischen Fakt und Meinung unterscheiden und Vokabular zur Quellenpruefung anwenden (source, headline, claim, evidence, to verify, reliable).

puzzles.prompt: The viral story has been reshared across the feed. Tap every version that shows a warning sign of fake news. Some reshares are calm and checkable - leave those alone.
puzzles.instruction: Mark all the posts that carry a fake-news red flag (no source, no date, all-caps panic).

posts.text: BREAKING!!! TOWN WATER IS POISONED - SHARE BEFORE THEY DELETE THIS!!!
Council confirms routine pipe maintenance on Mill Lane, 1 July 2026. Water is safe.
My cousin says the water thing is TRUE. No source but trust me, it's happening.
Scary headline going round. No author, no date, no idea where it started.
I called the water company today (1 July). They deny any contamination. Story checks out as false.

feedback.correct: Good detective work. The screaming all-caps post, the 'trust me' with no source, and the dateless anonymous repost are the fakes. The named, dated, checkable reports are fine.
feedback.wrong: Look closer. A calm post that names a source and a date isn't the problem. Flag the all-caps panic, the 'no source but trust me', and the anonymous dateless repost.

hints: Before you believe a post, ask a simple question: who actually said this, and when?
Real news names a source you can check and gives a date. Fake news shouts in capitals and skips both.
Flag the all-caps 'SHARE BEFORE THEY DELETE THIS', the 'no source but trust me', and the dateless anonymous repost. Leave the two named, dated reports.

puzzles.prompt: You zoom into the dramatic crowd photo attached to the story. It looks real - and it is - but a detail in the background gives it away. Click on the suspicious spot that proves this genuine photo is being recycled from an old, unrelated event. (Look for a poster in the background that shows an old year, '2016'.)

feedback.correct: Exactly. The poster reads '2016'. The photo is real but recycled - taken out of context and glued to a brand-new headline. That year on the poster blew its cover.
feedback.wrong: The crowd and the sky look normal because they are normal - the photo really is real. Look for a date on a poster in the background that proves it's old.

hints: The photo itself isn't faked. So what's wrong with it? Something small in the background dates it.
Scan the posters and signs behind the crowd, not the crowd itself. Look for a year.
Click the poster in the top-left that reads '2016' - that proves the photo is old and reused.

puzzles.prompt: You've proved the picture is genuine but lifted from another time and stuck onto a new headline. In one word, what has ALGO done to this image? (Type the single word that describes taking something away from its original setting.)

feedback.correct: Exactly. The photo has been taken out of context - genuine, but recycled to fit a lie.
feedback.wrong: Think about the '2016' on the poster. The picture is genuine but from another time. What word describes stripping an image from its original setting?

hints: The photo isn't faked. So what's wrong with it? It's been moved away from where it belongs.
A real, old picture stuck onto a new story is being used out of its original setting.
The answer describes taking a genuine image away from its original meaning - it's been used out of 'context'.

puzzles.prompt: Final step: run the suspicious story through your five-point fake-news checklist. For each finding, decide for yourself - does the story PASS this check, or is it a red flag that FAILS? Sort all five correctly to unlock the mission code.
items: Source: the post names no author and links to a site nobody has heard of.
Date: there is no date on the story anywhere.
Tone: it SHOUTS in capitals with panic words and rows of exclamation marks.
Cross-check: no other news outlet is reporting this at all.
Photo: the attached image is a genuine, unedited photograph.

feedback.correct: Four fails, one pass. Code accepted. Only the photo passes its own check - the image really is genuine (the trick was that it was recycled, not faked). No named source, no date, panic tone and no confirmation anywhere: this story must never be treated as true.
feedback.wrong: Look at each finding again. A missing source, a missing date, all-caps panic and no other outlet confirming it are all red flags - they FAIL. The photo is the odd one out: the image itself is real, so that single check PASSES.

hints: Ask of each finding: does this build trust, or destroy it? Trust = PASS, red flag = FAIL.
Four of the five findings are classic warning signs and fail. Only one thing about the story actually holds up.
No source, no date, panic tone and no cross-check all FAIL. The photo PASSES - the image is genuine, it was just misused earlier.

outro.speaker: Cipher
lines: That photo's real - but it's years old. ALGO glued it to a brand-new headline. Classic move. You caught it.
'Everyone's sharing it' is never the same as 'it's true'. You just proved that. Nice work, Agent.
